description: A pull-apart baguette slathered with black garlic butter and parmesan, then baked.
steps: Preheat the oven temperature to 400F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

In a small bowl, add the black raw garlic cloves and using a fork, mash the garlic until smooth like a paste. Then add the softened butter, parmesan cheese, salt, and pepper. Mix thoroughly to combine.
Grab the baguette and place on a cutting board. Cut slits into the bread about ½ inch apart, from one end of the bread to the other, without cutting all the way through.
Place the cut loaf on a large sheet of aluminum foil. Slather the black garlic butter between each slit, reaching the bottom of each slit, until all cuts are covered.
Wrap the baguette in the aluminum foil and place on the lined baking tray. Bake for 15 minutes.
Unwrap the bread from the aluminum foil and place on a wire rack to cool for about 5 minutes. Then place on a serving platter. Pull apart and enjoy!
